title: Riot Platforms Statistics 2025: Inside Growth Secrets
text: Riot Platforms produced 5,686 bitcoin in 2025, compared to 4,828 during 2024. The average cost to mine bitcoin, excluding depreciation, was $49,645 in 2025, as compared to $32,216 per bitcoin in 2024. The cost to mine a single bitcoin has risen 93% since the same period last year, primarily due to rise in average global network hash rate. All-in power cost for September 2025 was approximately 4.2 cents per kilowatt hour, up from 3.5 cents per kilowatt hour a year earlier. Despite increases, Riot's all-in power cost of 2.6 cents per kilowatt hour remains one of its most compelling advantages. For September 2025, average fleet efficiency hit 20.5 J/TH, improved from 23.2 J/TH a year earlier. Company achieved improved fleet efficiency at 21.1 J/TH, a 22% improvement year-over-year. Bitcoin mining operations generated $140.9 million in revenue in Q2 2025, with gross margin of 50%, slightly improved from 48% in Q1 2025. The company's strategic pivot to data centers and AI infrastructure is diversifying revenue streams while maintaining strong Bitcoin mining operations.
